source_issue.title: Document cypress commands, retrofit them to existing tests

summary_markdown:
```markdown
## What this issue is about
This issue focuses on improving the documentation of existing Cypress commands in the Plone Volto project. Currently, many Cypress commands are available, but their lack of documentation means not all tests use them efficiently. The goal is to document these commands and refactor tests to utilize them properly.

## What needs to be done
1. **Document all existing Cypress commands** found at [this location](https://github.com/plone/volto/tree/main/packages/volto/cypress/support).
2. **Refactor existing tests** to incorporate these documented commands. An example is updating to use `cy.toolbarSave()` where applicable.
3. There is a proposal to prefix Volto-specific commands (like `cy.voltoToolbarSave()`) for clarity, though this is still up for discussion.

## Helpful background
Cypress is a testing framework that allows for end-to-end testing of web applications. A good understanding of Cypress will be beneficial for this task.

## Good first steps
1. Familiarize yourself with [Cypress documentation](https://docs.cypress.io/).
2. Review the existing Cypress commands in the specified support directory.
3. Check the issue for any updates or ongoing discussions before starting. Coordinate with others if you decide to start working on this task to avoid duplication.
```
